title: Baalbeck
caption: The band at architectural scale: performance, ruin, crowd, and collective memory sharing one field.

title: More Than a Band
caption: Music became the entry point to a wider public language of desire, dissent, and belonging.

title: Beirut / 2008
caption: The project began among students in Beirut and developed through collective writing, performance, and self-production.

title: Counter-Public
caption: Songs, images, concerts, interviews, and audience exchange created a space that existing institutions did not provide.

title: Direct Relation
caption: Independent circulation allowed the audience to participate in the project before conventional gatekeepers recognized it.

title: Public Scale
caption: Regional and international visibility expanded the band’s reach while increasing the demands placed on its members and public.

title: Visibility / Protection
caption: The rail asks what public visibility can enable—and what it cannot protect on its own.

title: Ibn El Leil
caption: The 2015 album joined mourning, celebration, surveillance, and nightlife within a single nocturnal world.

title: Roman
caption: The 2017 film uses choreography, costume, and architecture to examine gendered power and representation.

title: Tiny Desk
caption: The intimate performance removes stage spectacle and foregrounds voice, arrangement, body, and room.

title: Stage Architecture
caption: Lighting, scenography, movement, and recognition turned touring into a portable spatial practice.

title: Rolling Stone
caption: Editorial portraiture placed the band inside a changing regional and international media imaginary.

title: Behind Visibility
caption: Touring, censorship, safety, press, market access, and audience trust shaped what could appear onstage.

title: HAIG PAPAZIAN / ROLE
caption: Co-founder, violinist, and creative director working across performance, visual identity, stage environments, and public media.
body: Co-founder | Violin | Creative direction | Visual identity | Stage environments

title: From Stage to Shelter
caption: Later spatial and institutional work carries forward a question sharpened here: how can a public also be supported?

title: Lil Watan
caption: The 2013 film places political language inside theatrical performance and a rapidly changing public sphere.

title: Fasateen
caption: The 2009 video records the handmade visual language of the band’s earliest period.

title: Raksit Leila
caption: The performance turns repetition, movement, and audience recognition into part of the song’s public life.

title: Cavalry
caption: The 2019 film frames confrontation, exhaustion, and endurance near the end of the band’s recording history.

title: Empty Stage
caption: The collective ended in 2022; its recordings, images, audiences, and unresolved questions remain active.
